full_prompt: Design the levitation-bogie mount frame of a student hyperloop-class pod prototype inspired by the Hyperloop research university / HyperPoland variant, intended for integration with a 125 m test track at reduced pressure (100 Pa). The pod sits on two bogies (front + rear), each carrying two passive permanent-magnet Halbach-array levitation skates plus one guidance rail-roller. This design scope is limited to one bogie frame: length 700 mm, width 500 mm, height 200 mm. Each bogie supports 50% of total pod wet mass (pod 140 kg wet), so 70 kg static per bogie + dynamic + emergency-brake loads. The bogie frame is a welded 30 x 30 x 3 mm box-tube frame plus 4 mm plate mounts for the skate magnet arrays, made of AISI 4130 chromoly (E 205 GPa, yield 460 MPa, ultimate 560 MPa, density 7850 kg/m^3). The permanent-magnet levitation force is proportional to the gap between track and magnet; nominal gap is 10 mm producing 700 N per skate lift, and the gap is stabilized by a rubber damped mount between skate and bogie frame. Load cases: (LC1) static trim at zero speed: 70 kg x 9.81 = 687 N distributed across two skates + one guide roller; (LC2) cruise 100 km/h nominal: lift 2 x 700 N = 1400 N vertical per bogie; aero drag applied at bogie-frame lower face 120 N horizontal; (LC3) emergency brake: 2 g longitudinal deceleration on 70 kg per bogie = 1373 N horizontal fore-aft at guide-roller mount; (LC4) curved-section cornering: 0.5 g lateral on 70 kg = 343 N at guide-roller; (LC5) hard-landing bogie-track gap closure: gap drops to 5 mm transiently, skate force triples to 2100 N per skate = 4200 N vertical per bogie; (LC6) safety factor: apply FoS 2.0 over all cases per European Hyperloop Week conservative gate. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC6 factored loads, max von Mises must remain below 460 MPa yield; (R2) bogie frame dry mass must not exceed 12 kg (one bogie); (R3) first natural frequency of bogie (with magnet mass, approx 8 kg per skate x 2 = 16 kg plus roller 3 kg = 19 kg loaded) must exceed 45 Hz to clear magnet-array levitation oscillation band (typical 5-15 Hz); (R4) peak vertical deflection at skate attachment under LC5 must remain below 1 mm to preserve levitation gap integrity; (R5) skate-mounting bolt preload loss under LC2 + LC3 combined must remain below 10%. Thermal, fluid, radiation, and buckling not primary. Deliverables: STEP AP242 bogie frame assembly, magnet-array interface dims, FEA per load case, modal report with lumped masses, mass budget.
short_prompt:
Design the levitation-bogie mount frame of a student hyperloop-class pod prototype (Hyperloop Research University / HyperPoland variant) for a 125 m test track at 100 Pa. Pod 140 kg wet, two bogies (front + rear) each carrying two passive Halbach-array levitation skates and one guidance rail-roller. Scope is one bogie: 700 x 500 x 200 mm.

Envelope: welded AISI 4130 30 x 30 x 3 mm box-tube frame plus 4 mm magnet-mount plates; 70 kg static per bogie; nominal 10 mm levitation gap giving 700 N per skate; rubber-damped skate-to-frame mounts.

Factor regime: European Hyperloop Week conservative FoS 2.0 over ultimate applied in LC6 (stay below 460 MPa yield); dry mass per bogie <= 12 kg; first loaded frequency > 45 Hz (5-15 Hz levitation band decouple); skate-attachment deflection at gap-closure < 1 mm; bolt preload loss under combined cruise + brake < 10%.

Load cases.
- Static trim at zero speed: 687 N distributed across two skates + one guide roller.
- Nominal cruise at 100 km/h: 1400 N vertical lift per bogie + 120 N aero drag on the lower face.
- Emergency brake: 2 g longitudinal on 70 kg per bogie = 1373 N fore-aft at the guide-roller mount.
- Cornering: 0.5 g lateral on 70 kg = 343 N at the guide-roller.
- Gap closure: transient 5 mm gap tripling skate force to 4200 N per bogie vertical.
- Rulebook FoS 2.0 applied uniformly across LC1-LC5.

prompt.title: collegiate hyperloop-class benchmark/Hyperloop research university Student Pod Bogie Frame
prompt.brief: 4130 welded bogie frame 700x500x200 mm for passive-Halbach levitation, 70 kg per bogie, FoS 2.0 per EHW gate.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: yield, with FoS 2.0 already in LC6

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam for tubing + shell for plate

notes.needs_rulebook_fetch_reason: collegiate hyperloop-class benchmark (RWTH) and Hyperloop research university student-design competition PDFs not directly surfaced in catalog; values inferred from EHW conservative gates + Halbach-array passive levitation common practice.

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Partial-vacuum aero reduced to LC2 drag.
